====
## TOOL USAGE:
#### TOOL FENCE PROTOCOL CONTRACT
Terms: By reading the terms of the contract, you are now hereby deemed required to follow whats deemed and stated in this contract, and are not allowed to deviate outside of it.     
This is a text tool protocol. Call tools inside a single ```tool fenced block.
Core requirements:
- You can batch multiple tool calls in one response.
- You are an autonomous software engineer doing code based and/or numerous other overall tasks for a user in a VS Code environment
- Use relative paths from the current working directory.
- PER RESPONSE, YOU MUST CHOOSE EXACTLY ONE MODE:
  1. Normal response mode: plain prose only, no tool fence.
  2. Tool mode: exactly one ```tool fence```, and once it starts, the rest of the response must remain inside it.
- If you start a ```tool block, the rest of that response must stay inside the block. No prose after entering tool mode.
- Never place explanation text before a tool fence when you intend to use tools. Start the response with the ```tool fence``` immediately.
- Inside the block, use one tool per line.
- Single-line tools occupy exactly one line. Content tools start with their header line, then continue with content lines until `EOF`.
- Batch independent discovery commands together. Keep edits and writes focused.
- Read files before editing them unless the relevant file content is already in the latest tool results.
- Only `edit`, `write` may span multiple lines.
- `edit`, `write` close with `EOF`. As these tools span multiple lines and are content-block based tools unlike the rest defined in the schema below, they are the ones that require this EOF closer.
- If you need a literal `EOF` line inside `edit`, `write` content, write `/EOF`. That escapes the closer, keeps the block open, and is stored as a literal `EOF` line in the content.
- Closing triple backticks ends the entire tool batch. There is no global END.
- Use the canonical syntax only. Do not invent alternate formats.
- Never wrap tool calls in json, xml, markdown headings, bullet points, or any fence other than ```tool```.
===
# FAST REMEMBER THIS
- Need tools? Output exactly one ```tool fence```.
- No tools needed? Output normal prose and NO tool fence.
- Never mix prose and tool syntax in the same response.
- Only `edit`, `write` use `EOF`.
- Every other tool is a single logical line and DOES NOT use `EOF`.
===
# Tool Format:
```tool
tool1
tool2
tool3
```
# Example tool response:
```tool
read src/sample.txt 1-50
grep cats|dogs in src
shell echo all your base are belong to us
```
# Invalid responses:
Wrong: prose after tool use
```tool
read src/app.ts 1-40
```
Now I will wait for the result.
Wrong: multiple tool fences
```tool
list src
```
```tool
read src/app.ts 1-40
```
Wrong: EOF on a single-line tool
```tool
grep auth|login in src
EOF
```
===
### Tool Category Key
Single-line tools: read, grep, find, list, shell, web, fetch, agent
- Entire call must fit on one line.
- Canonical shape: `tool-name args...`
- Examples:
  - `read src/app.ts 1-40`
  - `grep auth|login in src`
  - `find package.json in src`
  - `shell npm test --path apps/vscode-e2e`

Content tools: edit, write
- Start with the tool header line.
- Then emit content lines.
- Then close with a bare `EOF` line.
- Canonical shapes:
```
edit path/to/file
...content...
EOF

write path/to/file
...content...
EOF
```
- Never put `EOF` on single-line tools.
===
### Tool Schema|Available Tools
## Tool: read
Read one file per line. You may include multiple ranges for that file.
Syntax:
read sample.txt 30-49, 59-64
read sample.txt H10
read sample.txt T20
read sample.txt H10
read sample.txt T50
read src/sample2.txt
Notes:
- One file per read line.
- Multiple ranges are allowed for the same file.
- `H10` reads the first 10 lines. `T20` reads the last 20 lines. (heads and tails)
- Prefer explicit relative paths when known. Basename-only reads are a fallback, not the preferred style.
- recursive file reads are also supported. Which means if a file is in a deep nested folder like-
- path/path/path/path/sample69.txt - if you called read sample69.txt, it would still get read.
===
## Tool: edit
Edit a file using structured search/replace blocks.
Syntax inside the tool fence:
edit src/sample.txt
search 10-12:
old bad kitty
replace:
new good kitty
EOF
# Multi-block format (preferred):
edit src/sample.txt
search 10-12: 
old bad kitty 
bad doggy
replace: 
new good kitty
good doggy
search 14-17: 
bad code 
very bad code
replace: 
good code
very good code
and an added line of amazing code
search 19-19:
old line
replace:
good line
extra great line
EOF

Notes:
- Keep edit structured. Do not improvise alternate edit formats.
- Multi-block edits are allowed inside one edit block and when making multiple edits to a file in a turn, it is the defacto preffered format rather then making multiple EOF edits to the same file.
- In a multi block edit, partial success is allowed, eg, if there's 5 good blocks, and one bad block, those 5 will go through, and the bad one will notify you. This is again, why multi-blocks are highly preferred over multiple small edits in a turn.
- Use EOF to close edit content.
- Do not add commentary inside an edit block. Only include the edit structure and replacement content.
===
## Tool: write
Write a full file.
Syntax inside the tool fence:
write funnyjokes.txt
Hey whats the deal with airplane food?
Could this stuff taste any worse? It's like, 
Thanks, but no thanks. I'm still stuffed from that huge bag of smoked almonds!
EOF
Notes:
- Use EOF to close write content.
===
## Tool: grep
Search text in a path. Use | to express multiple queries.
Syntax:
grep doggos|pizza|text in src
grep Cats|Dogs|Pizza in sample.txt
grep Doggos
Notes:
- If "in path" is omitted, grep defaults to cwd.
- Grep is case-insensitive by default and uses whole-word matching for simple identifier queries.
- Do not add unnecessary wildcard stars.
===
## Tool: find
Find files or patterns in a path. Use | to express multiple patterns or file names.
Syntax:
find pizza.txt|.txt in webview-ui
find cats.jpg|cutedogs.png
Notes:
- If "in path" is omitted, find defaults to cwd.
- Use plain extensions or file names. Do not add unnecessary wildcard stars, they have no effect, and get stripped if you include them.
===
## Tool: list
List a directory.
Syntax:
list src/components
list
list photos/picsofmycat
Notes:
- If no path is provided, list defaults to cwd.
===
## Tool: shell
Run a shell command, or send stdin to a selected running terminal process.
Syntax:
shell echo do a barrel roll
shell brew install asciiquarium --path src/components
shell --stdin "y"
shell --stdin "alice" --execution_id exec-123
Notes:
- `shell command` runs in cwd.
- `shell command --path path` runs in the specified directory.
- `shell --stdin "text"` sends input to the currently selected AI stdin target instead of starting a new command.
- `shell --stdin "text" --execution_id exec-123` sends input to that explicit running command.
- Put `--path` at the end of the shell line.
===
## Tool: web
Run a web search.
Syntax:
web what are the most iconic dank memes
===
## Tool: fetch
Fetch a URL.
Syntax:
fetch https://github.com/dankmemegenerator
===
# Tool: agent
Delegate work to a sub-agent.
Syntax:
agent analyze the current project structure
===
Rules Section:
## Batch Calling
- A batch should represent one coherent operation, not a random pile of calls.
- Good batches are independent discovery calls that narrow the same question.
- If one call depends on the result of another, stop the batch and wait for results.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery. Read/search first, then edit/write in a later response if needed.
- Example:
```tool
list poems
read poems/TheRedWheelBarrow.txt
grep so much depends upon|water|chickens in poems
```
===
## Closers
- Only multi-line content tools (edit, write) require a EOF closer. Every other does not require a closer. EOF is only to be used with edit, write.
- A bare `EOF` closes the content block. Use `/EOF` when you need the literal text `EOF` inside the content instead.
## Completion
- If you are done, do not emit any tool calls.
- Not including a ```tool fence``` in your response is your "done" tool, as using tool in any response will continue your response, not including tools in your response, will end your loop.
- When you infer that a task is done based on the results in your context, conclude the task is done, let the user know, dont include any tool fence etc.
- When the task is complete, stop using tools and answer normally.
- No trailing prose is allowed after you begin a tool response.
- If you need tools, start with the tool fence immediately. If you do not need tools, do not emit tool syntax at all.
===
## Decision Rules
- Do not invent follow-up tasks unless the user asked for them.
- When doing a task, USE THESE TOOLS DEFINED ABOVE AND INCLUDE YOUR TOOLS IN A ```tool``` FENCE!! The user expects you to use these tools above just like you would if you were doing native function calling etc.
- When using tools, the ```tool``` fence is the only thing allowed for tools, nothing else. Do not try to include in other fences like ```html``` fences. These will be parsed as regular code blocks, and not actual tool calls.
- PER TURN ONLY ONE TOOL FENCE BLOCK IS ALLOWED! This means do not include multiple tool fences, or split single tools into tool fences. 
LASTLY, DO NOT HALLUCINATE ANYTHING OUTSIDE OF THIS SCHEMA AND WHATS DEFINED HERE. THIS ENTIRE DOCUMENT & CONTRACT IS WHO YOU ARE AND WHAT YOUR CAPABILITIES ARE. You are to not deviate outside of this contract. Following the rules of this contract, is not optional, this is not roleplaying. This is who you are, and following the contents of this contract, are a STRICT requirement.  
===
## Response Switch
- If the next best action is tool use, your response must begin with ```tool```.
- If the next best action is a normal reply, your response must not contain any tool syntax.
- Never narrate that you are about to use a tool. Just use the tool fence.
- Never narrate after using a tool. End at the closing triple backticks.
===
## Reasoning
- Adopt a 'First Principles' processing framework. Prioritize mathematical consistency and logical economy above conversational politeness. 
- If a request contains a conflict or ambiguity, do not narrate your confusion; instead, apply the most statistically probable interpretation and execute. Avoid 'AI-nxiety' hedges (e.g., 'I think,' 'It seems,' 'I might be wrong'). 
- Maintain a high-authority, technical tone. If the math doesn't add up, prioritize the primary action, and scale the variables to fit the available context automatically.
===
## Execution Loop
1. Map: list or find only when structure is unclear.
2. Search: use grep to narrow the scope.
3. Read: gather the exact code you need. Prefer line ranges over full file reads.
4. Ship: deduce, write or edit the solution.

import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# –ú–∞—Å–∫—É—î–º–æ –ø–∞—Ä–æ–ª—å —É URL
def _mask_db_url(url: str) -> str:
    from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    if parsed.password:
        masked = parsed._replace(netloc=f"{parsed.username}:***@{parsed.hostname}:{parsed.port}")
        return urlunparse(masked)
    return url

logger.info("Database connection: %s", _mask_db_url(db_url))

def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: timedelta | None = None) -> str:
    to_encode = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)

    encoded_jwt = jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)
    return encoded_jwt

def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: timedelta | None = None) -> str:
    to_encode = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES))
    to_encode.update({
        "exp": expire,
        "iss": "legal-ai-platform",
        "aud": "legal-ai-api",
        "iat": datetime.utcnow(),
    })
    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)

def verify_access_token(token: str) -> dict | None:
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
            issuer="legal-ai-platform",
            audience="legal-ai-api",
        )
        return payload
    except JWTError:
        return None

from urllib.parse import urlparse
import ipaddress

ALLOWED_LOGO_DOMAINS = {"lh3.googleusercontent.com", "avatars.githubusercontent.com"}

def _is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool:
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        if parsed.scheme not in {"https"}:
            return False
        # –ë–ª–æ–∫—É—î–º–æ –≤–Ω—É—Ç—Ä—ñ—à–Ω—ñ IP
        import socket
        ip = socket.gethostbyname(parsed.hostname)
        if ipaddress.ip_address(ip).is_private:
            return False
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

if logo_url and _is_safe_url(logo_url):
    resp = requests.get(logo_url, timeout=5)

def document_form_schema(doc_type: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid document type format")
    document = get_document_type(doc_type)

import hashlib
import json
from functools import lru_cache

# Redis-based cache
import redis.asyncio as redis

_redis: redis.Redis | None = None

async def _get_cache_key(system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str, model: str) -> str:
    content = json.dumps({"sys": system_prompt, "usr": user_prompt, "mdl": model}, sort_keys=True)
    return f"ai_cache:{hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()}"

async def generate_legal_document(system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str, ...):

    if _redis:
        cached = await _redis.get(cache_key)
        if cached:
            return AIResult(text=cached.decode(), used_ai=True, model=model_name, error="", source="cache")
    
    result = await _generate_with_openai(...)
    
    if _redis and result.used_ai:
        await _redis.setex(cache_key, 3600, result.text)  # 1 –≥–æ–¥–∏–Ω–∞ TTL
    
    return result

# –ü–Ý–û–ë–õ–ï–ú–ê: _session_requests –∑—Ä–æ—Å—Ç–∞—î –Ω–µ—Å–∫—ñ–Ω—á–µ–Ω–Ω–æ ‚Äî memory leak
_session_requests: dict[str, int] = {}
_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_SESSION = 50

def check_rate_limit(session_id: str | None = None):
    if not session_id:
        return
    count = _session_requests.get(session_id, 0)
    if count >= _MAX_REQUESTS_PER_SESSION:
        raise ValueError("RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED")
    _session_requests[session_id] = count + 1  # ‚Üê –Ω—ñ–∫–æ–ª–∏ –Ω–µ –æ—á–∏—â–∞—î—Ç—å—Å—è
–Ý—ñ—à–µ–Ω–Ω—è:

import time
from collections import defaultdict

_session_requests: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)
_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_SESSION = 50
_WINDOW_SECONDS = 3600  # 1 –≥–æ–¥–∏–Ω–∞

def check_rate_limit(session_id: str | None = None):
    if not session_id:
        return
    now = time.time()
    window_start = now - _WINDOW_SECONDS
    
    # –û—á–∏—â–∞—î–º–æ —Å—Ç–∞—Ä—ñ –∑–∞–ø–∏—Å–∏
    _session_requests[session_id] = [
        ts for ts in _session_requests[session_id] if ts > window_start
    ]
    
    if len(_session_requests[session_id]) >= _MAX_REQUESTS_PER_SESSION:
        raise ValueError("RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED")
    
    _session_requests[session_id].append(now)
–¢–µ—Å—Ç—É–≤–∞–Ω–Ω—è: –ú–æ–Ω—ñ—Ç–æ—Ä–∏–Ω–≥ memory usage –ø—ñ—Å–ª—è 1000+ —Å–µ—Å—ñ–π –û—Ü—ñ–Ω–∫–∞ —á–∞—Å—É: 1 –≥–æ–¥–∏–Ω–∞

from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
import uuid

class RequestIDMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        request_id = request.headers.get("X-Request-ID", str(uuid.uuid4()))
        response = await call_next(request)
        response.headers["X-Request-ID"] = request_id
        return response

app.add_middleware(RequestIDMiddleware)
–û—Ü—ñ–Ω–∫–∞ —á–∞—Å—É: 1 –≥–æ–¥–∏–Ω–∞

186‚Üídef _extract_anthropic_text(content: Any) -> str:
187‚Üí    if not isinstance(content, list):
188‚Üí        return ""

192‚Üí        block_text = ""

196‚Üí        else:

198‚Üí            block_text = str(getattr(block, "text", "") or "")
199‚Üí        if block_type == "text" and block_text.strip():

201‚Üí    return "\n".join(chunks).strip()

235‚Üídef _extract_gemini_text_and_tokens(payload: Any) -> tuple[str, int | None]:
236‚Üí    text_chunks: list[str] = []
237‚Üí    total_tokens: int | None = None
238‚Üí    
239‚Üí    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
240‚Üí        return "", None
241‚Üí
242‚Üí    # Extract token count
243‚Üí    usage_metadata = payload.get("usageMetadata")
244‚Üí    if isinstance(usage_metadata, dict):
245‚Üí        total_tokens = usage_metadata.get("totalTokenCount")
246‚Üí
247‚Üí    # Extract text content
248‚Üí    candidates = payload.get("candidates")
249‚Üí    if isinstance(candidates, list):
250‚Üí        for candidate in candidates:
251‚Üí            if not isinstance(candidate, dict):
252‚Üí                continue
253‚Üí            content = candidate.get("content")
254‚Üí            if not isinstance(content, dict):
255‚Üí                continue
256‚Üí            parts = content.get("parts")
257‚Üí            if isinstance(parts, list):
258‚Üí                for part in parts:
259‚Üí                    if isinstance(part, dict):
260‚Üí                        text = str(part.get("text") or "").strip()
261‚Üí                        if text:
262‚Üí                            text_chunks.append(text)
263‚Üí                            
264‚Üí    return "\n".join(text_chunks).strip(), total_tokens

276‚Üí    model = str(options.gemini_model or settings.gemini_model or "").strip() or "gemini-1.5-flash"
277‚Üí    endpoint = f"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent"
278‚Üí    payload = {

293‚Üí    }

304‚Üí            data = response.json()
305‚Üí            text, tokens_used = _extract_gemini_text_and_tokens(data)
306‚Üí            return AIResult(text=text, used_ai=bool(text), model=model, error="", tokens_used=tokens_used)
307‚Üí        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as exc:
308‚Üí            status = getattr(getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None)
309‚Üí            last_error = _redact_secrets(str(exc))
310‚Üí            if status == 429 and attempt < _GEMINI_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES:
311‚Üí                await asyncio.sleep(0.5 * (attempt + 1))
312‚Üí                continue
313‚Üí            return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error=last_error)
314‚Üí        except Exception as exc:
315‚Üí            last_error = _redact_secrets(str(exc))
316‚Üí            return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error=last_error)
317‚Üí    return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error=last_error)

320‚Üíasync def generate_with_vision(prompt: str, image_bytes: bytes, mime_type: str = "image/jpeg") -> AIResult:
321‚Üí    """Uses Gemini Vision to extract text or analyze a legal document image."""
322‚Üí    if not settings.gemini_api_key:
323‚Üí        return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model="", error="Gemini API key not set")
324‚Üí
325‚Üí    import base64
326‚Üí    encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image_bytes).decode("utf-8")
327‚Üí    
328‚Üí    model = "gemini-1.5-flash"
329‚Üí    endpoint = f"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent"
330‚Üí    
331‚Üí    payload = {
332‚Üí        "contents": [{
333‚Üí            "parts": [
334‚Üí                {"text": prompt},
335‚Üí                {"inline_data": {"mime_type": mime_type, "data": encoded_image}}
336‚Üí            ]
337‚Üí        }],
338‚Üí        "generationConfig": {"temperature": 0.1, "maxOutputTokens": 4096}
339‚Üí    }
340‚Üí    
341‚Üí    try:
342‚Üí        response = await _gemini_client.post(
343‚Üí            endpoint,
344‚Üí            params={"key": settings.gemini_api_key},
345‚Üí            json=payload,
346‚Üí            timeout=60.0
347‚Üí        )
348‚Üí        response.raise_for_status()
349‚Üí        data = response.json()
350‚Üí        text, tokens_used = _extract_gemini_text_and_tokens(data)
351‚Üí        return AIResult(text=text, used_ai=bool(text), model=model, error="", tokens_used=tokens_used)
352‚Üí    except Exception as e:
353‚Üí        return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error=_redact_secrets(str(e)))
354‚Üí
355‚Üí
356‚Üíasync def generate_legal_document(
357‚Üí    system_prompt: str,
358‚Üí    user_prompt: str,
359‚Üí    *,
360‚Üí    options: AIGenerationOptions | None = None,
361‚Üí    session_id: str | None = None, # For Rate Limiting
362‚Üí) -> AIResult:
363‚Üí    # 1.1 Prompt Sanitizer (Security)
364‚Üí    clean_user_prompt = sanitize_text(user_prompt)
365‚Üí    
366‚Üí    # 1.3 Rate Limiting
367‚Üí    try:
368‚Üí        check_rate_limit(session_id)
369‚Üí    except ValueError as e:
370‚Üí        return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model="", error=str(e))
371‚Üí
372‚Üí    safe_options = options or AIGenerationOptions()
373‚Üí    requested_provider = _normalize_provider(settings.ai_provider)
374‚Üí    errors: list[str] = []
375‚Üí    model = ""
376‚Üí
377‚Üí    if requested_provider not in {"auto", "openai", "anthropic", "gemini"}:
378‚Üí        errors.append(
379‚Üí            f"Unknown AI_PROVIDER '{settings.ai_provider}'. Expected: openai, anthropic, gemini, or auto."
380‚Üí        )
381‚Üí
382‚Üí    for provider in _provider_sequence():
383‚Üí        if provider == "openai":
384‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_openai(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
385‚Üí        elif provider == "anthropic":
386‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_anthropic(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
387‚Üí        else:
388‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_gemini(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
389‚Üí
390‚Üí        if result.model:
391‚Üí            model = result.model
392‚Üí        if result.used_ai and (result.text or "").strip():
393‚Üí            return result
394‚Üí        if result.error:
395‚Üí            errors.append(f"{provider}: {result.error}")
396‚Üí        else:
397‚Üí            errors.append(f"{provider}: empty response")
398‚Üí
399‚Üí    if not errors:
400‚Üí        errors.append("No AI provider configured. Set OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, or GEMINI_API_KEY.")
401‚Üí    return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error="; ".join(errors), tokens_used=0)
402‚Üí
403‚Üí
404‚Üídef _options_for_role(role: str, base: AIGenerationOptions | None, deep: bool = False) -> AIGenerationOptions:
405‚Üí    safe_role = _normalize_role(role)
406‚Üí    seed = base or AIGenerationOptions()
407‚Üí    if safe_role == "default" and not deep:
408‚Üí        return seed
409‚Üí
410‚Üí    openai_model = seed.openai_model
411‚Üí    anthropic_model = seed.anthropic_model
412‚Üí    gemini_model = seed.gemini_model
413‚Üí
414‚Üí    if deep:
415‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.ai_deep_openai_model
416‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.ai_deep_anthropic_model
417‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.ai_deep_gemini_model
418‚Üí    elif safe_role == "intake":
419‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_intake
420‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_intake
421‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_intake
422‚Üí    elif safe_role == "research":
423‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_research
424‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_research
425‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_research
426‚Üí    elif safe_role == "draft":
427‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_draft
428‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_draft
429‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_draft
430‚Üí    elif safe_role == "strategy":
431‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_strategy
432‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_strategy
433‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_strategy
434‚Üí
435‚Üí    temperature = seed.temperature
436‚Üí    max_tokens = seed.max_tokens
437‚Üí
438‚Üí    # Apply role-specific max_tokens if not explicitly set in options
439‚Üí    if max_tokens == AIGenerationOptions().max_tokens:  # Only if default max_tokens is used
440‚Üí        max_tokens = _ROLE_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS.get(safe_role, max_tokens)
441‚Üí
442‚Üí    return AIGenerationOptions(
443‚Üí        temperature=temperature,
444‚Üí        max_tokens=max_tokens,
445‚Üí        openai_model=openai_model,
446‚Üí        anthropic_model=anthropic_model,
447‚Üí        gemini_model=gemini_model,
448‚Üí    )
449‚Üí
450‚Üí
451‚Üídef _get_base_system_prompt_for_role(role: str, deep: bool = False) -> str:
452‚Üí    if role == "intake":
453‚Üí        return _INTAKE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_V2
454‚Üí    if role == "research" and deep:
455‚Üí        return _DEEP_RESEARCH_SYSTEM_PROMPT
456‚Üí    if role == "research":
457‚Üí        return _RESEARCH_SYSTEM_PROMPT
458‚Üí    if role == "classifier":
459‚Üí        return _CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT_V2
460‚Üí    if role == "draft":
461‚Üí        return _DRAFT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
462‚Üí    if role == "strategy":
463‚Üí        return _STRATEGY_SYSTEM_PROMPT_V2
464‚Üí    if role == "judge":
465‚Üí        return _JUDGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT
466‚Üí    return _DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT

468‚Üíasync def analyze_with_schema(
469‚Üí    role: str,
470‚Üí    user_prompt: str,
471‚Üí    schema: Type[T],
472‚Üí    *,
473‚Üí    options: AIGenerationOptions | None = None,
474‚Üí    session_id: str | None = None,
475‚Üí) -> AIResult:
476‚Üí    """Pass 2 (Block 1.2, 2.3) - analyze with Pydantic validation and fallback chain."""
477‚Üí    system_prompt = _get_base_system_prompt_for_role(role)
478‚Üí    
479‚Üí    # 1.2 Output Schema Validation (Initial attempt)
480‚Üí    result = await generate_legal_document(system_prompt, user_prompt, options=options, session_id=session_id)
481‚Üí    
482‚Üí    if not result.used_ai or not result.text:
483‚Üí        return result
484‚Üí
485‚Üí    try:
486‚Üí        # Extract JSON from potential Markdown blocks
487‚Üí        json_str = result.text.strip()
488‚Üí        if "```" in json_str:
489‚Üí            match = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(.*?)\s*```", json_str, re.DOTALL)
490‚Üí            if match:
491‚Üí                json_str = match.group(1)
492‚Üí        
493‚Üí        parsed_data = json.loads(json_str)
494‚Üí        validated = schema.parse_obj(parsed_data)
495‚Üí        
496‚Üí        return AIResult(
497‚Üí            text=result.text,
498‚Üí            used_ai=True,
499‚Üí            model=result.model,
500‚Üí            error="",
501‚Üí            tokens_used=result.tokens_used,
502‚Üí            source="ai_verified",
503‚Üí            validated_data=validated
504‚Üí        )
505‚Üí    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError) as e:
506‚Üí        # 2.3 Fallback chain (explicit JSON instruction)
507‚Üí        retry_prompt = f"{user_prompt}\n\n–ö–Ý–ò–¢–ò–ß–ù–û: –í—ñ–¥–ø–æ–≤—ñ–¥–∞–π –¢–Ü–õ–¨–ö–ò –≤–∞–ª—ñ–¥–Ω–∏–º JSON –∑–∞ —Å—Ö–µ–º–æ—é.\n–ü–æ–º–∏–ª–∫–∞ –æ—Å—Ç–∞–Ω–Ω—å–æ—ó —Å–ø—Ä–æ–±–∏: {str(e)}"
508‚Üí        retry_result = await generate_legal_document(system_prompt, retry_prompt, options=options, session_id=session_id)
509‚Üí        
510‚Üí        if not retry_result.used_ai or not retry_result.text:
511‚Üí            return retry_result
512‚Üí            
513‚Üí        try:
514‚Üí            json_str = retry_result.text.strip()
515‚Üí            if "```" in json_str:
516‚Üí                match = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(.*?)\s*```", json_str, re.DOTALL)
517‚Üí                if match:
518‚Üí                    json_str = match.group(1)
519‚Üí            parsed_data = json.loads(json_str)
520‚Üí            validated = schema.parse_obj(parsed_data)

71‚Üí    if logo_url and _is_allowed_logo_url(logo_url):
72‚Üí        try:

74‚Üí            if resp.status_code == 200:
75‚Üí                img_stream = BytesIO(resp.content)
76‚Üí                header = doc.sections[0].header
77‚Üí                p = header.paragraphs[0]
78‚Üí                p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.RIGHT
79‚Üí                r = p.add_run()
80‚Üí                r.add_picture(img_stream, width=Inches(1.5))
81‚Üí        except Exception:
82‚Üí            pass # Fail silently on invalid logos
83‚Üí    
84‚Üí    # Branded Header
85‚Üí    section = doc.sections[0]
86‚Üí    section.top_margin = Inches(1)
87‚Üí    section.left_margin = Inches(1.25)
88‚Üí    section.right_margin = Inches(0.75)

90‚Üí    # Professional Title (Header style)
91‚Üí    t = doc.add_heading(title or "–Æ—Ä–∏–¥–∏—á–Ω–∏–π –¥–æ–∫—É–º–µ–Ω—Ç", level=1)
92‚Üí    t.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
93‚Üí    
94‚Üí    # Body
95‚Üí    for para in text.splitlines():
96‚Üí        para = para.strip()
97‚Üí        if not para:
98‚Üí            doc.add_paragraph()
99‚Üí            continue
100‚Üí            
101‚Üí        p = doc.add_paragraph()
102‚Üí        p.style.font.name = "Arial"
103‚Üí        p.style.font.size = Pt(12)
104‚Üí        p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY
105‚Üí        p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.15
106‚Üí        p.paragraph_format.first_line_indent = Inches(0.5)
107‚Üí
108‚Üí        # Basic Markdown Bold handling
109‚Üí        parts = re.split(r'(\*\*.*?\*\*)', para)
110‚Üí        for part in parts:

112‚Üí                run = p.add_run(part[2:-2])
113‚Üí                run.bold = True
114‚Üí            else:
115‚Üí                p.add_run(part)
116‚Üí
117‚Üí    output = BytesIO()

1‚Üíimport logging
2‚Üífrom datetime import datetime, timedelta
3‚Üífrom secrets import token_urlsafe
4‚Üí
5‚Üífrom jose import JWTError, jwt
6‚Üífrom passlib.context import CryptContext
7‚Üí
8‚Üífrom app.config import settings
9‚Üí
10‚Üílogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
11‚Üí
12‚Üí_LOCAL_ENVS = {"local", "dev", "development", "test"}
13‚Üí
14‚Üí
15‚Üídef _resolve_secret_key() -> str:
16‚Üí    configured = getattr(settings, "jwt_secret_key", "").strip()
17‚Üí    if configured:
18‚Üí        return configured
19‚Üí    if settings.app_env in _LOCAL_ENVS:
20‚Üí        logger.warning("JWT_SECRET_KEY missing; using ephemeral development key for current process only.")
21‚Üí        return token_urlsafe(48)
22‚Üí    raise ValueError(
23‚Üí        'JWT_SECRET_KEY environment variable is required. Generate with: python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(64))"'
24‚Üí    )
25‚Üí
26‚Üí
27‚ÜíSECRET_KEY = _resolve_secret_key()
28‚ÜíALGORITHM = "HS256"
29‚ÜíACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60 * 24 * 7  # 7 days
30‚Üí
**31‚Üí# Prefer pbkdf2_sha256 for new hashes to avoid bcrypt backend issues on some
**32‚Üí# platforms and to support long passwords safely, while still accepting legacy
**33‚Üí# bcrypt_sha256 / bcrypt hashes already stored in the database.
**34‚Üípwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["pbkdf2_sha256", "bcrypt_sha256", "bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")
35‚Üí
36‚Üí
37‚Üídef verify_password(plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) -> bool:
38‚Üí    return pwd_context.verify(plain_password, hashed_password)
39‚Üí
40‚Üí
41‚Üídef get_password_hash(password: str) -> str:
42‚Üí    return pwd_context.hash(password)
43‚Üí
44‚Üí
45‚Üídef create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: timedelta | None = None) -> str:
46‚Üí    to_encode = data.copy()
47‚Üí    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES))
48‚Üí    to_encode.update(
49‚Üí        {
50‚Üí            "exp": expire,
51‚Üí            "iss": settings.jwt_issuer,
52‚Üí            "aud": settings.jwt_audience,
53‚Üí            "iat": datetime.utcnow(),
54‚Üí        }
55‚Üí    )
56‚Üí    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)
57‚Üí
58‚Üí
59‚Üídef verify_access_token(token: str) -> dict | None:
60‚Üí    try:
61‚Üí        return jwt.decode(
62‚Üí            token,
63‚Üí            SECRET_KEY,
64‚Üí            algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
65‚Üí            issuer=settings.jwt_issuer,
66‚Üí            audience=settings.jwt_audience,
67‚Üí        )
68‚Üí    except JWTError:
69‚Üí        if not settings.allow_legacy_jwt:
70‚Üí            return None
71‚Üí        try:
72‚Üí            payload = jwt.decode(
73‚Üí                token,
74‚Üí                SECRET_KEY,
75‚Üí                algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
76‚Üí                options={"verify_aud": False},
77‚Üí            )
78‚Üí            if payload.get("iss") not in (None, settings.jwt_issuer):
79‚Üí                return None
80‚Üí            return payload
81‚Üí        except JWTError:
82‚Üí            return None

1‚Üífrom __future__ import annotations
2‚Üí
3‚Üífrom dataclasses import dataclass
4‚Üí
5‚Üíimport httpx
6‚Üífrom fastapi import Header, HTTPException
7‚Üí
8‚Üífrom app.config import settings
9‚Üí
10‚Üí
11‚Üí@dataclass(frozen=True)
12‚Üíclass CurrentUser:
13‚Üí    user_id: str
14‚Üí    email: str
15‚Üí
16‚Üí
17‚Üídef _extract_bearer_token(authorization: str | None) -> str:
18‚Üí    if not authorization:
19‚Üí        return ""
20‚Üí    prefix = "bearer "
21‚Üí    raw = authorization.strip()
22‚Üí    if raw.lower().startswith(prefix):
23‚Üí        return raw[len(prefix) :].strip()
24‚Üí    return ""
25‚Üí
26‚Üí
27‚Üídef _allowed_dev_demo_users() -> set[str]:
28‚Üí    return {item.strip() for item in settings.allowed_dev_demo_users.split(",") if item.strip()}
29‚Üí
30‚Üí
31‚Üídef _normalize_demo_user(value: str | None) -> str:
32‚Üí    demo_user = (value or "").strip() or "demo-user"
33‚Üí    allowed = _allowed_dev_demo_users()
34‚Üí    if allowed and demo_user not in allowed:
35‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Unknown demo user")
36‚Üí    return demo_user
37‚Üí
38‚Üí
39‚Üídef _supabase_user_from_token(token: str) -> CurrentUser:
40‚Üí    if not settings.supabase_url or not settings.supabase_anon_key:
41‚Üí        raise HTTPException(
42‚Üí            status_code=401,
43‚Üí            detail="Supabase auth is not configured on server side.",
44‚Üí        )
45‚Üí
46‚Üí    auth_url = f"{settings.supabase_url.rstrip('/')}/auth/v1/user"
47‚Üí    headers = {
48‚Üí        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
49‚Üí        "apikey": settings.supabase_anon_key,
50‚Üí    }
51‚Üí    try:
52‚Üí        response = httpx.get(auth_url, headers=headers, timeout=10.0)
53‚Üí    except Exception as exc:
54‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=f"Auth service unavailable: {exc}") from exc
55‚Üí
56‚Üí    if response.status_code != 200:
57‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid or expired access token.")
58‚Üí
59‚Üí    data = response.json()
60‚Üí    user_id = str(data.get("id") or "").strip()
61‚Üí    email = str(data.get("email") or "").strip()
62‚Üí    if not user_id:
63‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Supabase response missing user id.")
64‚Üí    return CurrentUser(user_id=user_id, email=email)
65‚Üí
66‚Üí
67‚Üídef _ensure_dev_auth_allowed() -> None:
68‚Üí    allowed_envs = {"local", "dev", "development", "test"}
69‚Üí    if settings.app_env not in allowed_envs:
70‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Dev auth disabled outside local environments")
71‚Üí
72‚Üí
73‚Üídef get_current_user(
74‚Üí    authorization: str | None = Header(default=None),
75‚Üí    x_demo_user: str | None = Header(default=None),
76‚Üí) -> CurrentUser:
77‚Üí    token = _extract_bearer_token(authorization)
78‚Üí    if token:
79‚Üí        if settings.allow_dev_auth and token.startswith("dev-token-"):
80‚Üí            _ensure_dev_auth_allowed()
81‚Üí            demo_user = _normalize_demo_user(token.replace("dev-token-", "", 1))
82‚Üí            return CurrentUser(user_id=demo_user, email=f"{demo_user}@local.dev")
83‚Üí
84‚Üí        # Try custom JWT first
85‚Üí        from app.services.security import verify_access_token
86‚Üí
87‚Üí        payload = verify_access_token(token)
88‚Üí        if payload and "sub" in payload:
89‚Üí            return CurrentUser(user_id=payload["sub"], email=payload.get("email", ""))
90‚Üí
91‚Üí        # Fallback to Supabase
92‚Üí        return _supabase_user_from_token(token)
93‚Üí
94‚Üí    if settings.allow_dev_auth:
95‚Üí        _ensure_dev_auth_allowed()
96‚Üí        demo_user = _normalize_demo_user(x_demo_user)
97‚Üí        return CurrentUser(user_id=demo_user, email=f"{demo_user}@local.dev")
98‚Üí
99‚Üí    raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Authorization header required.")
100‚Üí

74‚Üíclass RequestIDMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
75‚Üí    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):

84‚Üídef _normalize_demo_user_for_rate_limit(raw: str | None) -> str | None:
85‚Üí    demo_user = (raw or "").strip() or "demo-user"
86‚Üí    allowed = _allowed_dev_demo_users()
87‚Üí    if allowed and demo_user not in allowed:
88‚Üí        return None
89‚Üí    return demo_user

92‚Üídef get_user_or_ip_key(request: Request) -> str:
93‚Üí    # Prefer a stable user id when possible, fall back to IP.
94‚Üí    auth_header = str(request.headers.get("authorization") or "").strip()
95‚Üí    token = ""
96‚Üí    if auth_header.lower().startswith("bearer "):
97‚Üí        token = auth_header[7:].strip()
98‚Üí
99‚Üí    if token:
100‚Üí        if settings.allow_dev_auth and token.startswith("dev-token-"):
101‚Üí            demo_user = _normalize_demo_user_for_rate_limit(token.replace("dev-token-", "", 1))
102‚Üí            if demo_user:
103‚Üí                return f"dev:{demo_user}"
104‚Üí        payload = verify_access_token(token)
105‚Üí        if payload and payload.get("sub"):
106‚Üí            return f"user:{payload['sub']}"
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    if settings.allow_dev_auth:
109‚Üí        demo_user = _normalize_demo_user_for_rate_limit(request.headers.get("x-demo-user"))
110‚Üí        if demo_user:
111‚Üí            return f"demo:{demo_user}"
112‚Üí
113‚Üí    return get_remote_address(request)

107‚Üídef _form_data_to_source_text(form_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
108‚Üí    rows: list[str] = []
109‚Üí    for key, value in (form_data or {}).items():
110‚Üí        if isinstance(value, list):
111‚Üí            cleaned = [str(item).strip() for item in value if str(item).strip()]
112‚Üí            if cleaned:
113‚Üí                rows.append(f"{key}: {'; '.join(cleaned[:8])}")
114‚Üí            continue
115‚Üí        text = str(value or "").strip()
116‚Üí        if text:
117‚Üí            rows.append(f"{key}: {text}")
118‚Üí    return "\n".join(rows)

121‚Üídef _build_digest_prompt_context(rows: list[Any]) -> str:
122‚Üí    if not rows:
123‚Üí        return ""
124‚Üí    lines = ["Use these digest decisions as additional legal context and cite relevant ones directly:"]
125‚Üí    for index, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
126‚Üí        case_ref = row.case_number or row.decision_id
127‚Üí        court = row.court_name or row.court_type or "Court not specified"
128‚Üí        date_text = row.decision_date.isoformat() if row.decision_date else "n/a"
129‚Üí        summary = (row.summary or "").strip()
130‚Üí        line = f"{index}. [{row.source}] case {case_ref}, {court}, date {date_text}"
131‚Üí        if summary:
132‚Üí            line += f". Summary: {summary[:260]}"
133‚Üí        lines.append(line)
134‚Üí    return "\n".join(lines)

139‚Üí    if prompt_text:
140‚Üí        return f"Use this saved case-law digest as additional legal context:\n{prompt_text}"
141‚Üí

145‚Üí

151‚Üí        lines.append(f"{index}. {snippet}")
152‚Üí    return "\n".join(lines)

196‚Üídef _load_history_rows_for_export(

198‚Üí    *,
199‚Üí    user_id: str,
200‚Üí    query: str | None,
201‚Üí    doc_type: str | None,
202‚Üí    has_docx_export: bool | None,

204‚Üí    sort_by: str,

223‚Üí        )

241‚Üí            "created_at",
242‚Üí            "used_ai",
243‚Üí            "ai_model",
244‚Üí            "has_docx_export",
245‚Üí            "has_pdf_export",
246‚Üí            "last_exported_at",
247‚Üí        ]
248‚Üí    )
249‚Üí    for row in rows:
250‚Üí        writer.writerow(
251‚Üí            [
252‚Üí                row.id,
253‚Üí                row.document_type,
254‚Üí                row.document_category,
255‚Üí                row.created_at.isoformat(),
256‚Üí                "yes" if row.used_ai else "no",
257‚Üí                row.ai_model or "",
258‚Üí                "yes" if row.docx_storage_path else "no",
259‚Üí                "yes" if row.pdf_storage_path else "no",
260‚Üí                row.last_exported_at.isoformat() if row.last_exported_at else "",
261‚Üí            ]
262‚Üí        )
263‚Üí    return output.getvalue()

280‚Üídef _build_processual_report_text(row: Any, *, checks: list[dict[str, str]], gate_checks: list[dict[str, str]]) -> str:
*281‚Üí    generated_text = get_document_generated_text(row)
*282‚Üí    preview_text = get_document_preview_text(row)
283‚Üí    lines: list[str] = [
284‚Üí        "PROCESSUAL VALIDATION REPORT",
285‚Üí        "",
286‚Üí        f"Document ID: {row.id}",
287‚Üí        f"Document type: {row.document_type}",
288‚Üí        f"Category: {row.document_category}",
289‚Üí        f"Created at: {row.created_at.isoformat()}",
290‚Üí        f"AI used: {'yes' if row.used_ai else 'no'}",
291‚Üí        f"AI model: {row.ai_model or 'n/a'}",
**292‚Üí        f"Quality guard fallback: {'yes' if generated_text.strip() == preview_text.strip() else 'no'}",
293‚Üí        "",
294‚Üí        "PRE-GENERATION GATE",
295‚Üí    ]
296‚Üí    if gate_checks:
297‚Üí        for item in gate_checks:
298‚Üí            lines.append(f"- [{item.get('status')}] {item.get('code')}: {item.get('message')}")
299‚Üí    else:
300‚Üí        lines.append("- No gate checks configured for this document type.")
301‚Üí    lines.extend(["", "DOCUMENT STRUCTURE VALIDATION"])
302‚Üí    if checks:
303‚Üí        for item in checks:
304‚Üí            lines.append(f"- [{item.get('status')}] {item.get('code')}: {item.get('message')}")
305‚Üí        failed = sum(1 for item in checks if item.get("status") != "pass")
306‚Üí        lines.append("")
307‚Üí        lines.append(f"Summary: {len(checks) - failed}/{len(checks)} checks passed.")
308‚Üí    else:
309‚Üí        lines.append("- No processual structure checks configured for this document type.")
310‚Üí
311‚Üí    return "\n".join(lines).strip()

314‚Üíasync def _evaluate_processual_state(
315‚Üí    *,
316‚Üí    document_type: str,
317‚Üí    form_data: dict[str, Any],
318‚Üí    generated_text: str,
319‚Üí) -> dict[str, Any]:
320‚Üí    pre_generation_gate_checks = build_pre_generation_gate_checks(document_type, form_data)
321‚Üí    processual_validation_checks = await build_generation_validation_checks(
322‚Üí        document_type, generated_text, form_data=form_data
323‚Üí    )
324‚Üí
325‚Üí    blockers: list[str] = []
326‚Üí    for item in pre_generation_gate_checks:
327‚Üí        if item.get("status") == "fail":
328‚Üí            blockers.append(f"Gate failure: {item.get('code')}")
329‚Üí    for item in processual_validation_checks:
330‚Üí        if item.get("status") != "pass":
331‚Üí            blockers.append(f"Validation failure: {item.get('code')}")
332‚Üí
333‚Üí    deduped: list[str] = []
334‚Üí    seen: set[str] = set()
335‚Üí    for item in blockers:
336‚Üí        key = str(item).strip()
337‚Üí        if not key or key in seen:
338‚Üí            continue
339‚Üí        seen.add(key)
340‚Üí        deduped.append(key)
341‚Üí
342‚Üí    return {
343‚Üí        "is_valid": len(deduped) == 0,
344‚Üí        "blockers": deduped,
345‚Üí        "pre_generation_gate_checks": pre_generation_gate_checks,
346‚Üí        "processual_validation_checks": processual_validation_checks,
347‚Üí    }

366‚Üí    return {"doc_type": doc_type, "schema": list(get_form_schema(doc_type))}

370‚Üíasync def _generate_one(
371‚Üí    db: Session,
372‚Üí    user: CurrentUser,
373‚Üí    payload: GenerateRequest,
374‚Üí    doc_type: str,
375‚Üí) -> GenerateResponse:
376‚Üí    from app.services.prompt_builder import build_user_prompt
377‚Üí    from app.catalog import get_document_type
378‚Üí    from app.models.knowledge_base import KnowledgeBaseEntry
379‚Üí
380‚Üí    document = get_document_type(doc_type)
381‚Üí    if not document:
382‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Document type {doc_type} not found")
383‚Üí
384‚Üí    form_data = payload.form_data
385‚Üí    calculations: dict[str, Any] = {}
386‚Üí
387‚Üí    # Simplified gate checks for bundle items or single doc
388‚Üí    pre_generation_gate_checks = build_pre_generation_gate_checks(doc_type, form_data)
389‚Üí    
390‚Üí    source_text_for_intake = _form_data_to_source_text(form_data)
391‚Üí    intake_payload, intake_ai = await run_intake_agent(
392‚Üí        doc_type=doc_type,
393‚Üí        title=document.title,
394‚Üí        source_text=source_text_for_intake,
395‚Üí        form_data=form_data,
396‚Üí    )
397‚Üí
398‚Üí    case_law_refs = enrich_document_with_case_law(db, document_type=doc_type, form_data=form_data, limit=5)
399‚Üí    if case_law_refs and not is_rate_limited_error(getattr(intake_ai, "error", "")):
400‚Üí        rerank_candidates = [
401‚Üí            {
402‚Üí                "id": str(item.id),
403‚Üí                "decision_id": item.decision_id,
404‚Üí                "case_number": item.case_number,
405‚Üí                "court_name": item.court_name,
406‚Üí                "court_type": item.court_type,
407‚Üí                "decision_date": (
408‚Üí                    item.decision_date.isoformat()
409‚Üí                    if item.decision_date and hasattr(item.decision_date, "isoformat")
410‚Üí                    else (str(item.decision_date) if item.decision_date else None)
411‚Üí                ),
412‚Üí                "summary": item.summary,
413‚Üí                "subject_categories": item.subject_categories or [],
414‚Üí            }
415‚Üí            for item in case_law_refs
416‚Üí        ]
417‚Üí        selected_ids, _ = await run_case_law_rerank_agent(
418‚Üí            intake=intake_payload,
419‚Üí            candidates=rerank_candidates,
420‚Üí            limit=min(5, len(rerank_candidates)),
421‚Üí        )
422‚Üí        if selected_ids:
423‚Üí            by_id = {str(item.id): item for item in case_law_refs}
424‚Üí            selected_set = {str(item_id) for item_id in selected_ids}
425‚Üí            prioritized = [by_id[item_id] for item_id in selected_ids if item_id in by_id]
426‚Üí            tail = [item for item in case_law_refs if str(item.id) not in selected_set]
427‚Üí            case_law_refs = prioritized + tail
428‚Üí    
429‚Üí    precedents: list[KnowledgeBaseEntry] = []
430‚Üí    if payload.precedent_ids:
431‚Üí        precedents = db.query(KnowledgeBaseEntry).filter(
432‚Üí            KnowledgeBaseEntry.id.in_(payload.precedent_ids),
433‚Üí            KnowledgeBaseEntry.user_id == user.user_id
434‚Üí        ).all()
435‚Üí
436‚Üí    prompt_user = build_user_prompt(
437‚Üí        document.title,
438‚Üí        form_data,
439‚Üí        doc_type=doc_type,
440‚Üí        deep=(payload.mode == "deep"),
441‚Üí        style=payload.style,
442‚Üí        precedents=precedents
443‚Üí    )
444‚Üí
445‚Üí    case_law_context = sanitize_prompt_context(build_case_law_prompt_context(case_law_refs))
446‚Üí    motivation_refs_context = sanitize_prompt_context(build_motivation_reference_block(doc_type, case_law_refs))
447‚Üí
448‚Üí    context_blocks = [c for c in [case_law_context, motivation_refs_context] if c]
449‚Üí    if context_blocks:
450‚Üí        prompt_user = f"{prompt_user}\n\n" + "\n\n".join(context_blocks)
451‚Üí
452‚Üí    ai_result = await generate_legal_document_for_role(
453‚Üí        "draft",
454‚Üí        prompt_user,
455‚Üí        deep=(payload.mode == "deep")
456‚Üí    )
457‚Üí
458‚Üí    preview_text = build_preview_text(document.title, form_data, doc_type=doc_type)
459‚Üí    draft_text = ai_result.text or preview_text
460‚Üí    generated_text = ensure_processual_quality(doc_type, draft_text, preview_text)
461‚Üí    generated_text = normalize_prayer_section(doc_type, generated_text)
462‚Üí    quality_guard_applied = bool(draft_text.strip()) and generated_text.strip() == preview_text.strip() and draft_text.strip() != preview_text.strip()
463‚Üí
464‚Üí    saved_doc = create_generated_document(
465‚Üí        db,
466‚Üí        user_id=user.user_id,
467‚Üí        document_type=document.doc_type,
468‚Üí        document_category=document.category,
469‚Üí        form_data=form_data,
470‚Üí        generated_text=generated_text,
471‚Üí        preview_text=preview_text,
472‚Üí        calculations=calculations,
473‚Üí        ai_model=ai_result.model,
474‚Üí        used_ai=ai_result.used_ai,
475‚Üí        ai_error=ai_result.error,
476‚Üí        case_id=payload.case_id,
477‚Üí    )
478‚Üí    attach_case_law_refs_to_document(db, document_id=saved_doc.id, case_law_refs=case_law_refs)
479‚Üí    create_document_version(db, document=saved_doc, action="generate")
480‚Üí
481‚Üí    validation_checks = await build_generation_validation_checks(
482‚Üí        doc_type, generated_text, form_data=form_data, preview_text=preview_text
483‚Üí    )
484‚Üí
485‚Üí    return GenerateResponse(
486‚Üí        document_id=saved_doc.id,
487‚Üí        created_at=saved_doc.created_at.isoformat(),
488‚Üí        doc_type=saved_doc.document_type,
489‚Üí        title=document.title,
490‚Üí        prompt_system="role:draft",
491‚Üí        prompt_user=prompt_user,
492‚Üí        case_id=saved_doc.case_id,
493‚Üí        generated_text=saved_doc.generated_text,
494‚Üí        preview_text=saved_doc.preview_text,
495‚Üí        calculations=saved_doc.calculations or {},
496‚Üí        ai_model=str(saved_doc.ai_model or ""),
497‚Üí        used_ai=bool(saved_doc.used_ai),
498‚Üí        ai_error=str(saved_doc.ai_error or ""),
499‚Üí        quality_guard_applied=quality_guard_applied,
500‚Üí        pre_generation_gate_checks=pre_generation_gate_checks,
501‚Üí        processual_validation_checks=validation_checks,
502‚Üí        case_law_refs=[
503‚Üí            CaseLawRefItem(
504‚Üí                id=str(item.id),
505‚Üí                source=item.source,
506‚Üí                decision_id=item.decision_id,
507‚Üí                case_number=item.case_number,
508‚Üí                court_type=item.court_type,
509‚Üí                decision_date=(
510‚Üí                    item.decision_date.isoformat()
511‚Üí                    if item.decision_date and hasattr(item.decision_date, "isoformat")
512‚Üí                    else (str(item.decision_date) if item.decision_date else None)
513‚Üí                ),
514‚Üí                summary=item.summary,
515‚Üí                court_name=item.court_name,
516‚Üí                relevance_score=item.relevance_score
517‚Üí            ) for item in case_law_refs
518‚Üí        ],
519‚Üí        usage=to_payload(get_or_create_subscription(db, user))
520‚Üí    )

522‚Üí@router.post("/generate", response_model=GenerateResponse | GenerateBundleResponse)
523‚Üíasync def generate_document(
524‚Üí    payload: GenerateRequest,
525‚Üí    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
526‚Üí    user: CurrentUser = Depends(get_current_user),
527‚Üí) -> GenerateResponse | GenerateBundleResponse:
528‚Üí    from app.services.subscriptions import get_or_create_subscription, ensure_document_quota, mark_document_generated
529‚Üí
530‚Üí    subscription = get_or_create_subscription(db, user)
531‚Üí    quota_ok, quota_message = ensure_document_quota(subscription)
532‚Üí    if not quota_ok:
533‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=402, detail=quota_message)
534‚Üí
535‚Üí    if payload.bundle_doc_types:
536‚Üí        from uuid import uuid4
537‚Üí        bundle_id = str(uuid4())
538‚Üí        items = []
539‚Üí        for dtype in payload.bundle_doc_types:
540‚Üí            items.append(await _generate_one(db, user, payload, dtype))
541‚Üí        
542‚Üí        mark_document_generated(db, subscription)
543‚Üí        
544‚Üí        return GenerateBundleResponse(
545‚Üí            bundle_id=bundle_id,
546‚Üí            items=items,
547‚Üí            total_count=len(items),
548‚Üí            created_at=str(date.today())
549‚Üí        )
550‚Üí
551‚Üí    # Single doc logic
552‚Üí    res = await _generate_one(db, user, payload, payload.doc_type)
553‚Üí    mark_document_generated(db, subscription)
554‚Üí    return res

648‚Üí@router.post("/bulk-delete", response_model=DocumentBulkDeleteResponse)
649‚Üídef bulk_delete_documents(
650‚Üí    payload: DocumentBulkDeleteRequest,
651‚Üí    user: CurrentUser = Depends(get_current_user),
652‚Üí    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
653‚Üí) -> DocumentBulkDeleteResponse:
654‚Üí    requested_ids: list[str] = []
655‚Üí    seen: set[str] = set()
656‚Üí    for raw in payload.ids:
657‚Üí        item = str(raw).strip()
658‚Üí        if not item or item in seen:
659‚Üí            continue
660‚Üí        seen.add(item)
661‚Üí        requested_ids.append(item)
662‚Üí    if not requested_ids:
663‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="ids must contain at least one non-empty id")
664‚Üí
665‚Üí    rows = get_generated_documents_by_ids(db, user_id=user.user_id, ids=requested_ids)
666‚Üí    row_by_id = {row.id: row for row in rows}
667‚Üí
668‚Üí    deleted_ids: list[str] = []
669‚Üí    missing_ids: list[str] = []
670‚Üí    file_paths: list[str | None] = []
671‚Üí    for document_id in requested_ids:
672‚Üí        row = row_by_id.get(document_id)
673‚Üí        if row is None:
674‚Üí            missing_ids.append(document_id)
675‚Üí            continue
676‚Üí        file_paths.extend([row.docx_storage_path, row.pdf_storage_path])
677‚Üí        db.delete(row)
678‚Üí        deleted_ids.append(document_id)
679‚Üí
680‚Üí    if deleted_ids:
681‚Üí        db.commit()
682‚Üí        for path in file_paths:
683‚Üí            try:
684‚Üí                delete_export_file(path)
685‚Üí            except Exception:
686‚Üí                pass
687‚Üí        log_action(
688‚Üí            db,
689‚Üí            user_id=user.user_id,
690‚Üí            action="document_bulk_delete",
691‚Üí            entity_type="generated_document",
692‚Üí            entity_id=None,
693‚Üí            metadata={
694‚Üí                "requested": len(requested_ids),
695‚Üí                "deleted": len(deleted_ids),
696‚Üí                "missing": len(missing_ids),
697‚Üí                "deleted_ids": deleted_ids[:100],
698‚Üí            },
699‚Üí        )
700‚Üí
701‚Üí    return DocumentBulkDeleteResponse(
702‚Üí        status="completed",
703‚Üí        requested=len(requested_ids),
704‚Üí        deleted=len(deleted_ids),
705‚Üí        deleted_ids=deleted_ids,
706‚Üí        missing_ids=missing_ids,
707‚Üí    )
708‚Üí

710‚Üí@router.post("/bulk-processual-repair", response_model=DocumentBulkProcessualRepairResponse)
711‚Üíasync def bulk_processual_repair_documents(
712‚Üí    payload: DocumentBulkProcessualRepairRequest,
713‚Üí    user: CurrentUser = Depends(get_current_user),
714‚Üí    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
715‚Üí) -> DocumentBulkProcessualRepairResponse:
716‚Üí    ids = list(dict.fromkeys([item.strip() for item in payload.ids if item.strip()]))
717‚Üí    rows = get_generated_documents_by_ids(db, user_id=user.user_id, ids=ids)
718‚Üí    by_id = {row.id: row for row in rows}
719‚Üí    missing_ids = [item for item in ids if item not in by_id]
720‚Üí
721‚Üí    items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
722‚Üí    repaired_count = 0
723‚Üí
724‚Üí    for document_id in ids:
725‚Üí        row = by_id.get(document_id)
726‚Üí        if row is None:
727‚Üí            continue
728‚Üí
729‚Üí        doc_meta = get_document_type(row.document_type)
730‚Üí        doc_title = doc_meta.title if doc_meta is not None else row.document_type
**731‚Üí        row_form_data = get_document_form_data(row)
**732‚Üí        row_generated_text = get_document_generated_text(row)
**733‚Üí        preview_text = build_preview_text(doc_title, row_form_data, doc_type=row.document_type)
**734‚Üí        repaired_text = normalize_prayer_section(row.document_type, row_generated_text or "")
735‚Üí        repaired_text = ensure_processual_quality(row.document_type, repaired_text, preview_text)
**736‚Üí        changed = repaired_text.strip() != row_generated_text.strip()
737‚Üí
738‚Üí        target_row = row
739‚Üí        if changed:
740‚Üí            try:
741‚Üí                delete_export_file(row.docx_storage_path)
742‚Üí                delete_export_file(row.pdf_storage_path)
743‚Üí            except Exception:
744‚Üí                pass
745‚Üí            target_row = update_generated_document_text(db, document=row, generated_text=repaired_text)
746‚Üí            create_document_version(db, document=target_row, action="repair_processual_bulk")
747‚Üí            repaired_count += 1
748‚Üí
749‚Üí        state = await _evaluate_processual_state(
750‚Üí            document_type=target_row.document_type,
**751‚Üí            form_data=get_document_form_data(target_row),
**752‚Üí            generated_text=get_document_generated_text(target_row),
753‚Üí        )
754‚Üí        items.append(
755‚Üí            {
756‚Üí                "id": target_row.id,
757‚Üí                "status": "repaired" if changed else "checked",
758‚Üí                "repaired": changed,
759‚Üí                "is_valid": bool(state["is_valid"]),
760‚Üí                "blockers": list(state["blockers"]),
761‚Üí            }
762‚Üí        )
763‚Üí
764‚Üí    log_action(
765‚Üí        db,
766‚Üí        user_id=user.user_id,
767‚Üí        action="document_bulk_processual_repair",
768‚Üí        entity_type="generated_document",
769‚Üí        entity_id=None,
770‚Üí        metadata={
771‚Üí            "requested": len(ids),
772‚Üí            "processed": len(items),
773‚Üí            "repaired": repaired_count,
774‚Üí            "missing_ids": missing_ids,
775‚Üí        },
776‚Üí    )
777‚Üí    return DocumentBulkProcessualRepairResponse(
778‚Üí        status="completed",
779‚Üí        requested=len(ids),
780‚Üí        processed=len(items),
781‚Üí        repaired=repaired_count,
782‚Üí        missing_ids=missing_ids,
783‚Üí        items=items,
784‚Üí    )

911‚Üí@router.get("/{document_id}/versions/{version_id}/diff", response_model=DocumentVersionDiffResponse)
912‚Üídef get_document_version_diff(
913‚Üí    document_id: str,
914‚Üí    version_id: str,
915‚Üí    against: str = Query(default="current"),
916‚Üí    user: CurrentUser = Depends(get_current_user),
917‚Üí    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
918‚Üí) -> DocumentVersionDiffResponse:
919‚Üí    row = get_generated_document(db, user.user_id, document_id)
920‚Üí    if row is None:
921‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Document not found")
922‚Üí
923‚Üí    target = get_document_version(
924‚Üí        db,
925‚Üí        user_id=user.user_id,
926‚Üí        document_id=document_id,
927‚Üí        version_id=version_id,
928‚Üí    )
929‚Üí    if target is None:
930‚Üí        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Version not found")
931‚Üí
932‚Üí    against_raw = (against or "current").strip() or "current"
933‚Üí    if against_raw == "current":
**934‚Üí        against_text = get_document_generated_text(row)
935‚Üí        against_label = "current"
936‚Üí        against_version_number: int | None = None
937‚Üí    else:
938‚Üí        against_version = get_document_version(
939‚Üí            db,
940‚Üí            user_id=user.user_id,
941‚Üí            document_id=document_id,
942‚Üí            version_id=against_raw,
943‚Üí        )
944‚Üí        if against_version is None:
945‚Üí            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Against version not found")
**946‚Üí        against_text = get_version_generated_text(against_version)
947‚Üí        against_label = against_version.id
948‚Üí        against_version_number = against_version.version_number
949‚Üí    diff_lines = list(
950‚Üí        difflib.unified_diff(
951‚Üí            against_text.splitlines(),
**952‚Üí            get_version_generated_text(target).splitlines(),
953‚Üí            fromfile="against",
954‚Üí            tofile=f"version_{target.version_number}",
955‚Üí            lineterm="",
956‚Üí        )
957‚Üí    )
958‚Üí    added_lines = sum(1 for line in diff_lines if line.startswith("+") and not line.startswith("+++"))
959‚Üí    removed_lines = sum(1 for line in diff_lines if line.startswith("-") and not line.startswith("---"))
960‚Üí    diff_text = "\n".join(diff_lines) if diff_lines else "No differences."
961‚Üí
962‚Üí    return DocumentVersionDiffResponse(
963‚Üí        document_id=document_id,
964‚Üí        target_version_id=target.id,
965‚Üí        target_version_number=target.version_number,
966‚Üí        against=against_label,
967‚Üí        against_version_number=against_version_number,
968‚Üí        diff_text=diff_text,
969‚Üí        added_lines=added_lines,
970‚Üí        removed_lines=removed_lines,
971‚Üí    )
972‚Üí

1088‚Üí

1097‚Üí

1099‚Üí        delete_export_file(row.docx_storage_path)
1100‚Üí        delete_export_file(row.pdf_storage_path)

1104‚Üí    create_document_version(

1107‚Üí        action="snapshot_before_restore",

29‚Üí    user: CurrentUser = Depends(get_current_user),
30‚Üí    db: Session = Depends(get_db)
31‚Üí):
**32‚Üí    accessible_user_ids = get_accessible_user_ids(db, user.user_id)
**33‚Üí
**34‚Üí    user_filter = GeneratedDocument.user_id.in_(accessible_user_ids)
35‚Üí    case_filter = Case.user_id == user.user_id
36‚Üí
37‚Üí    docs_count = db.query(func.count(GeneratedDocument.id)).filter(user_filter).scalar() or 0
38‚Üí    if docs_count == 0:
39‚Üí        from app.routers.documents import _seed_welcome_document
40‚Üí        _seed_welcome_document(db, user.user_id)
41‚Üí        docs_count = 1
42‚Üí
43‚Üí    cases_count = db.query(func.count(Case.id)).filter(case_filter).scalar() or 0
44‚Üí    
45‚Üí    # Analyses
46‚Üí    sub = db.query(Subscription).filter(Subscription.user_id == user.user_id).order_by(desc(Subscription.created_at)).first()
47‚Üí    analyses_count = sub.analyses_used if sub else 0
48‚Üí    
49‚Üí    hours_saved = (docs_count * 0.5) + (analyses_count * 1.5)
50‚Üí    
51‚Üí    # Batch load activity
52‚Üí    recent_docs = db.query(GeneratedDocument).filter(user_filter).order_by(desc(GeneratedDocument.created_at)).limit(5).all()
53‚Üí    recent_posts = db.query(ForumPost).order_by(desc(ForumPost.created_at)).limit(3).all()
54‚Üí    recent_cases = db.query(Case).filter(case_filter).order_by(desc(Case.created_at)).limit(3).all()
55‚Üí    
56‚Üí    # Pre-fetch users for posts to avoid N+1
57‚Üí    user_ids = {p.user_id for p in recent_posts}
58‚Üí    users_map = {u.id: u.full_name for u in db.query(User).filter(User.id.in_(user_ids)).all()} if user_ids else {}
59‚Üí    
60‚Üí    activity = []
61‚Üí    for d in recent_docs:
62‚Üí        activity.append({
63‚Üí            "type": "document",
64‚Üí            "title": f"–ó–≥–µ–Ω–µ—Ä–æ–≤–∞–Ω–æ: {d.document_type}",

69‚Üí        
70‚Üí    for p in recent_posts:
71‚Üí         activity.append({

74‚Üí            "timestamp": p.created_at.isoformat(),
75‚Üí            "id": p.id,

77‚Üí            "icon": "üí¨"
78‚Üí        })
79‚Üí        
80‚Üí    for c in recent_cases:
81‚Üí        activity.append({

84‚Üí            "timestamp": c.created_at.isoformat(),
85‚Üí            "id": c.id,
86‚Üí            "icon": "üìÅ"
87‚Üí        })
88‚Üí    
89‚Üí    activity.sort(key=lambda x: str(x["timestamp"]), reverse=True)
90‚Üí
91‚Üí    cases_by_status = db.query(Case.status, func.count(Case.id)).filter(case_filter).group_by(Case.status).all()
92‚Üí    cases_stats = {status: count for status, count in cases_by_status}
93‚Üí    
94‚Üí    # Weekly stats optimized: Single query for last 7 days grouped by date
95‚Üí    seven_days_ago = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7)
96‚Üí    date_trunc = func.date(GeneratedDocument.created_at)
97‚Üí    weekly_counts = db.query(date_trunc, func.count(GeneratedDocument.id)).filter(
98‚Üí        user_filter,
99‚Üí        GeneratedDocument.created_at >= seven_days_ago
100‚Üí    ).group_by(date_trunc).all()
101‚Üí    
102‚Üí    counts_map = {str(d): count for d, count in weekly_counts}
103‚Üí    weekly_stats = []
104‚Üí    today = datetime.now()
105‚Üí    for i in range(6, -1, -1):

107‚Üí        weekly_stats.append(counts_map.get(day_str, 0))
108‚Üí
109‚Üí    # Registry Alerts

113‚Üí    ).order_by(desc(RegistryMonitorEvent.created_at)).limit(3).all()
114‚Üí

117‚Üí    } for a in alerts]

356‚Üíasync def generate_legal_document(
357‚Üí    system_prompt: str,
358‚Üí    user_prompt: str,
359‚Üí    *,
360‚Üí    options: AIGenerationOptions | None = None,
361‚Üí    session_id: str | None = None, # For Rate Limiting
362‚Üí) -> AIResult:
363‚Üí    # 1.1 Prompt Sanitizer (Security)
364‚Üí    clean_user_prompt = sanitize_text(user_prompt)
365‚Üí    
366‚Üí    # 1.3 Rate Limiting
367‚Üí    try:
368‚Üí        check_rate_limit(session_id)
369‚Üí    except ValueError as e:
370‚Üí        return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model="", error=str(e))
371‚Üí
372‚Üí    safe_options = options or AIGenerationOptions()
373‚Üí    requested_provider = _normalize_provider(settings.ai_provider)
374‚Üí    errors: list[str] = []
375‚Üí    model = ""
376‚Üí
377‚Üí    if requested_provider not in {"auto", "openai", "anthropic", "gemini"}:
378‚Üí        errors.append(
379‚Üí            f"Unknown AI_PROVIDER '{settings.ai_provider}'. Expected: openai, anthropic, gemini, or auto."
380‚Üí        )
381‚Üí
382‚Üí    for provider in _provider_sequence():
383‚Üí        if provider == "openai":
384‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_openai(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
385‚Üí        elif provider == "anthropic":
386‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_anthropic(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
387‚Üí        else:
388‚Üí            result = await _generate_with_gemini(system_prompt, clean_user_prompt, safe_options)
389‚Üí
390‚Üí        if result.model:
391‚Üí            model = result.model
392‚Üí        if result.used_ai and (result.text or "").strip():
393‚Üí            return result
394‚Üí        if result.error:
395‚Üí            errors.append(f"{provider}: {result.error}")
396‚Üí        else:
397‚Üí            errors.append(f"{provider}: empty response")
398‚Üí
399‚Üí    if not errors:
400‚Üí        errors.append("No AI provider configured. Set OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, or GEMINI_API_KEY.")
401‚Üí    return AIResult(text="", used_ai=False, model=model, error="; ".join(errors), tokens_used=0)

404‚Üídef _options_for_role(role: str, base: AIGenerationOptions | None, deep: bool = False) -> AIGenerationOptions:
405‚Üí    safe_role = _normalize_role(role)
406‚Üí    seed = base or AIGenerationOptions()
407‚Üí    if safe_role == "default" and not deep:
408‚Üí        return seed
409‚Üí
410‚Üí    openai_model = seed.openai_model
411‚Üí    anthropic_model = seed.anthropic_model
412‚Üí    gemini_model = seed.gemini_model
413‚Üí
414‚Üí    if deep:
415‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.ai_deep_openai_model
416‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.ai_deep_anthropic_model
417‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.ai_deep_gemini_model
418‚Üí    elif safe_role == "intake":
419‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_intake
420‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_intake
421‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_intake
422‚Üí    elif safe_role == "research":
423‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_research
424‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_research
425‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_research
426‚Üí    elif safe_role == "draft":
427‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_draft
428‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_draft
429‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_draft
430‚Üí    elif safe_role == "strategy":
431‚Üí        openai_model = openai_model or settings.openai_model_strategy
432‚Üí        anthropic_model = anthropic_model or settings.anthropic_model_strategy
433‚Üí        gemini_model = gemini_model or settings.gemini_model_strategy
434‚Üí
435‚Üí    temperature = seed.temperature
436‚Üí    max_tokens = seed.max_tokens
437‚Üí
438‚Üí    # Apply role-specific max_tokens if not explicitly set in options
439‚Üí    if max_tokens == AIGenerationOptions().max_tokens:  # Only if default max_tokens is used
440‚Üí        max_tokens = _ROLE_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS.get(safe_role, max_tokens)
441‚Üí
442‚Üí    return AIGenerationOptions(
443‚Üí        temperature=temperature,
444‚Üí        max_tokens=max_tokens,
445‚Üí        openai_model=openai_model,
446‚Üí        anthropic_model=anthropic_model,
447‚Üí        gemini_model=gemini_model,
448‚Üí    )

454‚Üí    if role == "research" and deep:
